evidence: On January 14, 2025, we completed the acquisition of the Silicon Carbide Junction Field-Effect Transistor ('SiC JFET') technology business from Qorvo US, Inc...for $118.8 million in cash
On October 27, 2025, we completed the acquisition of rights to Vcore power technologies...The total purchase consideration is up to $144 million

our intelligent power technologies enable energy efficiency in a market in which energy needs are growing at an exponential rate, and AI data center operators are focused on reducing energy consumption
PSG provides a broad portfolio of discrete, module, and integrated semiconductor devices designed to enable high efficiency and high power conversion across AI data centers
increased revenue of $15.1 million in other end-markets which include AI data centers

Our power technologies designed for AI use may not capture market share as expected...Our failure to commercialize new technologies that can power AI data centers in a timely manner...could result in a loss of market share, unanticipated costs and inventory obsolescence
our association with AI infrastructure could expose us to reputational harm, stakeholder criticism or increased regulatory scrutiny, even if we do not control the design or use of AI systems

Our use of AI tools could also result in the loss of confidential information or intellectual property...subject us to risks related to intellectual property infringement or misappropriation, data privacy, cybersecurity

capex_mentions.quote: acquisition of the SiC JFET technology business from Qorvo US, Inc...for $118.8 million in cash
capex_mentions.context: January 2025 acquisition to address power supply units for AI data centers

capex_mentions.quote: acquisition of rights to Vcore power technologies...total purchase consideration is up to $144 million
capex_mentions.context: October 2025 acquisition enhancing power management portfolio relevant to AI data centers; $72M contingent on revenue milestones through 2030

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: The $118.8M and $144M figures are M&A/acquisition spend, not traditional capex or dedicated AI infrastructure build-out; no separate R&D or capex budget specifically for AI is quantified, so score reflects disclosed acquisition amounts as the closest proxy

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI data center revenue contribution is partially quantified ($15.1M incremental PSG revenue) but AI data center is bundled in 'Other' segment ($1.24B total); no standalone AI data center revenue line or product-level metrics disclosed; score reflects deployed products with some operating detail but limited precision

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk section material is present and covers multiple named, company-specific risk mechanisms (market share loss, reputational harm, regulatory scrutiny, IP/data risks); stops short of quantified risk exposure or named governance/oversight bodies, hence scored 4 not 5
